puzzle.title: The Voice of Wisdom
puzzle.intro: A gentle voice of wisdom once shared counsel that transcends time. This figure, blessed with deep understanding, passed on invaluable lessons that remain a beacon for all who yearn for guidance. Can you unravel the identity behind these enduring words?
clues: I was bestowed with a profound blessing from Allah, a gift that enlightened my heart and mind.
I turned to my beloved child, desiring to share the most valuable treasure I possessed.
I warned that the gravest injustice is to set up partners with the One true God.
I reminded that even a weight of a mustard seed, hidden inside a rock or beyond the skies, will be brought to account by Allah.
I instructed to be dutiful to parents, though obedience is only in matters of righteousness.
I taught simple manners: walk with humility on the earth and moderate your voice; for the harshest sound is the braying of donkeys.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Luqman
Al-Khidr
Prophet Sulayman
Dhul-Qarnayn
Uzair

trial.label: Trial / Event
options: Offering profound advice to his child
Building an ark to survive a great flood
Enduring a severe illness with patience
Journeying to the ends of the earth
Receiving divine tablets on a mountain
trial.answer: Offering profound advice to his child

location.label: Key Element
options: The advice to his son
The burning bush
The staff of Musa
The golden calf
The iron gates

outcome.label: Outcome
options: His advice was immortalized in divine scripture
He was granted prophethood and a new law
He became a mighty ruler over a vast kingdom
He was raised to the heavens without death
He received a direct revelation from the angel
outcome.answer: His advice was immortalized in divine scripture

puzzle.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا لُقْمَـٰنَ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ ٱشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌۭ
puzzle.verse: And We had certainly given Luqmān wisdom [and said], "Be grateful to Allāh." And whoever is grateful is grateful for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever denies [His favor] - then indeed, Allāh is Free of need and Praiseworthy. (31:12)
